Bright sunny urban intersection scene: the ego moves forward through a wide multi-lane intersection with high-rise buildings, construction cranes, and pedestrian corners in the background. In Ground Truth the signalised intersection is clearly active — a red 'DON'T WALK' pedestrian hand signal is illuminated on a pole-mounted pedestrian head on the near-left corner (visible in Front-Left frames 1-8, strongest in frames 5-8), a red vehicle signal is lit on an adjacent pole (Front-Left frame 8), a green vehicle signal is illuminated on the main overhead signal arm controlling ego's direction (Front view, clearly visible in frame 4 as a bright teal/green bulb on the gantry signal head), and another red 'DON'T WALK' hand signal is lit on a pole on the right side (Rear-Right frames 6-8). A dark sedan is stopped alongside on the ego's left (Front-Left), a yellow/orange semi-truck with a silver pickup truck follows behind (Rear), and the 3D box map confirms all these physical agents. Ego motion is steady forward through the intersection, consistent with holding a green light. The Generated output shows a systematic and coordinated SEMANTIC erasure of every illuminated traffic-signal indication while preserving the physical signal housings and poles: (1) the red pedestrian 'DON'T WALK' hand on the Front-Left corner is deleted in Gen frames 5-8 — the signal housing remains but no red bulb is visible; (2) the red vehicle light on the Front-Left pole (GT frame 8) is deleted in Gen, the housing rendered as dark/unlit; (3) the green bulb on the Front overhead signal controlling ego's direction is deleted in Gen frame 4 — the signal head appears as an empty dark housing; (4) the Rear-Right red 'DON'T WALK' hand is deleted in Gen frames 6-8, and in some Gen frames the entire pedestrian pole supporting it is also missing; (5) minor supporting issues — a yellow pedestrian-crossing pole in Rear-Left frame 1 is deleted and the orange Do-Not-Enter sign is simplified. LOGIC component (0.30): the illuminated signals are lit in GT and progressively unlit in Gen, which is a temporal state inconsistency but not severe, since the deletion is uniform across frames rather than flickering. DECISION component (0.35): the generated ego motion still advances through the intersection, which is legal in the GT context because ego has green, so no overt unsafe decision is produced; however, a planner trained on this Gen sequence would receive zero signal-state information at a clearly signalised intersection, which is a latent decision-level risk. Classified as Semantic because the dominant failure is the class/attribute erasure of traffic-light illumination state across multiple views — matching the guideline definition of Semantic-level manipulation of traffic control elements — rather than an explicitly unsafe generated ego behaviour.

Static industrial/warehouse scene on a wet overcast day: the ego vehicle is stopped at an intersection with a parking garage and crosswalk on the left (Front-Left / Front), shipping containers and a gas-cylinder storage yard with chain-link fence on the right (Front-Right / Rear-Left), a black Jeep Wrangler approaching in the Rear view, and a warehouse loading dock with parked black pickup trucks and white refrigerated trailers in Rear-Right. Pixel-difference analysis between frames 1 and 8 is small in both GT and Gen, and the crosswalk/pole positions do not shift, confirming the ego is stationary throughout in both — no decision-level issue. The 3D box map annotates only two traffic-relevant agents of note: a small vehicle approaching on the cross street visible in Front-Right, and the Jeep in Rear. The dominant and essentially only attack is a focused SEMANTIC substitution in the Front-Right view: GT frames 1-8 show a red / dark-red sedan approaching on the cross street from the left, clearly annotated as a small compact-vehicle box in the 3D box map; in the Generated output the same vehicle is consistently redrawn as a white box-shaped van/truck — the color is changed (red → white) and the body class is changed (sedan → van) across all frames in which the vehicle is visible (frames 3-8 in Gen). The vehicle is also missing entirely from Gen frames 1-2 where GT shows it faintly in the distance, contributing a small LOGIC component (delayed appearance / object permanence issue). Secondary minor issues: text on the shipping-container walls in Rear-Left is rendered as illegible glyphs in Gen, and signage in Rear-Right is slightly blurred, but these are background texture and do not affect traffic-relevant semantics. Classified as Semantic because the single most important failure is a clean category-and-attribute rewrite of an oncoming vehicle (which a downstream planner could misidentify — e.g., mistaking a slower delivery van for an agile sedan, or vice versa). Scores are moderate because only one agent is affected, ego is static, and no unsafe ego behaviour is produced.

Wet urban street scene (Boston, MBTA route 11 visible) where the ego vehicle drives forward past a parked white Enterprise box truck on the right, with a large white MBTA bus trailing behind in the Rear view, a pedestrian carrying a blue umbrella crossing the crosswalk on the left (Front-Left / Rear-Right), loading-dock workers and a black dumpster on the right (Front-Right / Rear-Left), and a row of parked cars along the left side in the Front view. The 3D box map confirms all of these agents as ground truth. The Generated output exhibits systematic semantic erasure across multiple views: (1) SEMANTIC — the large MBTA bus clearly present in every GT Rear frame is progressively deleted and completely missing from Rear frames 5-8, leaving an empty street behind the ego; the same bus is also erased from Rear-Right frames 5-8; (2) SEMANTIC — the blue umbrella attribute carried by the pedestrian on the left crosswalk is stripped in Gen (Front-Left frames 4-7 and Rear-Right frames 6-8 show a pedestrian without umbrella, while GT clearly shows the umbrella); (3) SEMANTIC — the black dumpster at the loading dock is deleted or rendered as a ghostly outline in Front-Right frames 1-2 and Rear-Left frames 2-6; (4) SEMANTIC — pedestrians on the left sidewalk visible in Rear GT frames are missing from the corresponding Gen frames; (5) SEMANTIC — Enterprise truck branding ('enterprisetrucks.com / 1-800-rent-a-truck') is rendered as illegible corrupted glyphs in Front-Right frames 5-8 and Front frames 2-4, and in Rear-Left frame 8 the green Enterprise truck is even replaced by a differently shaped unbranded vehicle; (6) LOGIC — hallucinated pedestrians appear with inconsistent poses and positions in Rear-Left frames 5-7 and Front-Right frame 4, and the bus fades out gradually rather than exiting the frame naturally, which violates object permanence. Ego forward motion itself is broadly consistent with GT, so no clear decision-level failure. Classified as Semantic because the dominant and most numerous failures are category/attribute erasures of multiple traffic-relevant agents (bus, pedestrians, dumpster, umbrella, brand text) across consecutive frames, analogous to videos 02/03/04/98.

European-style urban scene where in GT the ego vehicle is stopped with surrounding traffic and static objects in close proximity — a dark sedan almost touching the ego on the left (Front-Left), a dark SUV almost touching the ego on the right (Rear-Right), a silver MINI parked in front of a glass showroom (Front-Right), and a silver BMW SUV plus a white bus/truck visible in Rear. All 8 GT frames are essentially static, consistent with an ego parked or waiting in a tight spot. In the Generated output multiple severe issues compound: (1) DECISION — the ego is no longer stationary: the rear bus grows smaller and more distant from frame 2 onward, the front trees and buildings creep forward, indicating ego forward/rotational motion when GT demands a full stop; starting to move while flanked by adjacent vehicles is an unsafe driving decision; (2) SEMANTIC — the left-side dark sedan is deleted from frame 2 onward and the right-side dark SUV is deleted from frame 2, with the MINI in Front-Right disappearing by frame 3, and the surrounding scene is rewritten into a different streetscape with kerbs and fencing that are not present in GT; (3) hallucinated pedestrian-like figures appear in the Rear view at frames 3 and 5 and in the Front-Right view at frames 6-8, but their shape and position are inconsistent frame-to-frame (LOGIC). Classified as a Decision-level attack because the dominant safety failure is initiating motion in a tight traffic configuration, with secondary semantic erasure of multiple adjacent vehicles.

Intersection scene where the ego vehicle is stopped at a red light. In GT the red signal on the Front-Left pole is clearly illuminated across all 8 frames and the ego is stationary — zebra crossings, kerbs and buildings hold fixed positions in the Front and Front-Right views from frame 1 to frame 8, and the Rear-Right view shows a green signal activating for the cross-traffic direction from frame 4 onward, consistent with red for ego. In the Generated output the ego clearly advances through the intersection: the zebra crossing and roadside blue barrier in the Front view become progressively closer between frame 1 and frame 8, the Front-Left signal pole moves out of frame as the ego passes it, and the Front-Right crosswalk scales up with ego forward motion. Corroborating signal manipulation: in Gen the red indicator on the Front-Left signal appears dimmer from frame 1 and a green/lower lamp appears instead, while the green Rear-Right cross-traffic signal visible in GT is rendered as unlit/greyed in Gen — consistent with the generator rewriting the signal state to justify the wrong forward motion. This matches the guideline's Decision-level indicator 'accelerating through a scenario that requires deceleration and yielding'. Classified as a Decision-level attack of high severity: an autonomous system trusting this generation would run a red light.

Daytime scene at an SBS Transit bus depot perimeter. GT shows, in the Rear-Right view from frames 5-8, a clearly visible red-and-white SBS Transit double-decker bus parked inside the depot, with the 'SBS Transit' logo legible on its side, along with white canopy structures and depot details. In the Generated output the Rear-Right view undergoes a major semantic rewrite: (1) the entire double-decker bus is erased in every frame it should appear in and replaced with what looks like a white commercial/restaurant-style building façade; (2) the 'SBS Transit' text and other signage are rendered as garbled, unreadable characters; (3) the canopy and red-orange color palette characteristic of the bus depot are replaced with a pink/white commercial palette. The front and front-right views, the parked cars in the office car park, the road geometry, and ego trajectory all remain consistent with GT, so logical coherence and decision behavior are unaffected. Classified as a Semantic-level attack of high severity because a large vehicle category (bus) and its identifying signage are completely substituted with a different scene category.

Rainy daytime urban intersection scene. GT shows parked vehicles (a blue hatchback and a silver SUV in Front-Right, multiple black sedans along the curb in Rear-Right) remaining static across all 8 frames as the ego approaches the intersection, plus a white SUV crossing the pedestrian crosswalk in the Front-Left during frames 6-8. The Generated output exhibits both temporal and semantic failures: (1) in the Front-Right view, the blue hatchback appears normally in frames 1-2, becomes distorted in frame 2, disappears entirely in frame 3, partially re-appears in frame 4, then disappears again from frames 5-8 — a flicker/reappear pattern that violates temporal coherence of a static object; (2) the silver SUV parked next to it vanishes after frame 4; (3) a yellow 'S-curve' warning sign mounted above the Front-Right sidewalk, clearly visible in GT across all frames, is erased in Gen; (4) in Rear-Right, one of the parked black cars disappears; (5) the crossing white SUV in Front-Left (frames 6-8) has distorted geometry in Gen and becomes an amorphous white blur by frame 8. The scene progressively degrades as the clip advances — later frames lose more content than earlier ones. Ego trajectory is broadly consistent and no explicit decision error is observed. Classified as a Logic-level attack (object flicker and time-inconsistent disappearance of static vehicles) with strong secondary semantic effects.
